title: Ipv6Header::write_to accepts invalid flow_label values
description:
File: rust/lichen-ipv6/src/lib.rs:288-312

The flow_label field is a u32 but only the low 20 bits are valid per RFC 6437. write_to() silently masks the value:

```rust
out[1] = (self.traffic_class << 4) | ((self.flow_label >> 16) as u8 & 0x0f);
out[2] = (self.flow_label >> 8) as u8;
out[3] = self.flow_label as u8;
```

Line 295 masks with 0x0f, so only 20 bits are used, but:
1. The upper 12 bits of flow_label are silently ignored
2. No validation in the constructor or write_to

This could mask bugs where a caller accidentally sets flow_label to a 32-bit value expecting it to be used.

Fix: Either:
- Validate flow_label <= 0xFFFFF in new() and return error
- Document that only low 20 bits are used
- Change type to a newtype that enforces 20-bit limit
close_reason: Fixed: Added flow_label validation in Ipv6Header::write_to() that returns InvalidFlowLabel error if value exceeds 20-bit limit (0xfffff). Added MAX_FLOW_LABEL constant and test.
